data.content:
Root cause assessment
- This is both a doctor heuristic bug and a doctor --fix bug.
- checkPromptGuidance derives canonical expected profile IDs from workflow descriptors, but compares them against the raw FOOLERY_PROMPT_PROFILE value from AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md. Legacy aliases like beads-coarse-human-gated are normalized elsewhere in the workflow layer, but not here, so doctor can warn on guidance that is semantically valid.
- applyFix("prompt-guidance") selects PROMPT_KNOTS.md or PROMPT_BEATS.md by repo type and appends the file verbatim. That is not profile-aware: knots templates currently omit FOOLERY_PROMPT_PROFILE entirely, and beads templates currently hardcode the legacy beads-coarse-human-gated alias. A fix can therefore write guidance that still fails the next doctor run.
- The prompt-guidance fix path is append-only. When a file already has a managed guidance block but the profile is missing or mismatched, rerunning fix creates duplicate blocks instead of updating the managed one. The checker also searches the whole file for the first FOOLERY_PROMPT_PROFILE match rather than scoping to the managed block, so stale appended content can keep influencing diagnostics.

Implementation plan
1. Add a small shared prompt-guidance helper (likely a new src/lib/prompt-guidance.ts, or equivalent helper section in doctor.ts if kept local) to:
   - extract the managed block between FOOLERY_GUIDANCE_PROMPT_START / END
   - render a guidance block from the existing template content while injecting a canonical FOOLERY_PROMPT_PROFILE line for the requested expected profile
   - upsert the managed block by replacing an existing block or appending once when missing
2. Reuse canonical profile normalization from the workflow layer instead of duplicating alias logic.
   - Export the existing normalizeProfileId helper from src/lib/workflows.ts, or add an exported wrapper with a clearer name, so doctor code can compare canonical profile IDs against canonical expected profile IDs.
3. Update checkPromptGuidance in src/lib/doctor.ts to:
   - parse the profile from the extracted managed block instead of the whole file
   - normalize both actual and expected profile IDs before comparison
   - keep warning when the block exists but no profile marker is present, but stop flagging accepted legacy aliases as mismatches
4. Update applyFix("prompt-guidance") in src/lib/doctor.ts to:
   - stop using raw appendFile for every case
   - render the chosen template with ctx.expectedProfile injected dynamically
   - replace an existing managed block in-place when present, or append once when absent
   - keep repo-type template selection (PROMPT_KNOTS vs PROMPT_BEATS), but make the rendered block profile-aware so semiauto/autopilot repos are fixed correctly
5. Add regression tests:
   - src/lib/__tests__/doctor.test.ts: legacy alias in the managed block is accepted when the canonical expected profile is semiauto; unrelated text elsewhere in the file does not affect parsing
   - src/lib/__tests__/doctor-applyfix.test.ts: prompt-guidance fix replaces mismatched guidance instead of duplicating it; knots guidance gets an injected FOOLERY_PROMPT_PROFILE; beads guidance is rewritten to the requested canonical profile
   - src/lib/__tests__/doctor-coverage.test.ts: still warn when the managed block has no profile line; still warn for a genuinely wrong canonical profile
   - update any stale test mocks that currently expect legacy promptProfileId values from listWorkflows so tests reflect runtime canonical descriptors

Files likely touched
- src/lib/doctor.ts
- src/lib/workflows.ts
- src/lib/__tests__/doctor.test.ts
- src/lib/__tests__/doctor-applyfix.test.ts
- src/lib/__tests__/doctor-coverage.test.ts
- optionally a new src/lib/prompt-guidance.ts helper if that keeps parsing/rendering logic isolated

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: medium
- Main risk: duplicating profile alias rules in multiple places. Mitigation: export and reuse the canonical workflow normalization helper.
- Main behavior risk: rewriting the wrong file region when guidance blocks are malformed or duplicated. Mitigation: scope replacement strictly to the managed start/end markers and cover duplicate/mismatch cases in tests.
- Follow-up risk outside this knot: the shell-level foolery prompt / setup prompt appenders still use static templates and append-only behavior, so they may need a parity pass later if we want manual prompt insertion to be fully consistent with doctor.

Hierarchy
- No child knots needed; this fits in a single implementation pass.
